detail: docs/tools/<name>.md is a pre-build design plan, not a living spec: once a tool ships, freeze the doc with a historical banner pointing at the tool's own README/CLAUDE.md; only cross-tool contract changes get a follow-up edit here. Reverted the --handoff detail added to my-reporter.md in favor of this convention

Add ClaudeCLIEngine to mythings.engine: shells out to the claude CLI in headless print mode (-p --output-format json --tools ""), no SDK dependency, reuses existing claude auth. Never raises — CLI failure or bad JSON degrades to EngineResult(text=""), same contract shape as NoopEngine's empty reply, so every tool's existing summarize-degrades-gracefully path covers it for free

Chose shelling out to the claude CLI over the anthropic SDK or raw HTTP: zero new dependency (matches the dependency-free-runtime rule), and it reuses the caller's existing claude authentication instead of requiring a separately managed API key. Declined --bare mode despite its lower token cost because it forces ANTHROPIC_API_KEY auth, which would break that property for OAuth/keychain users

ClaudeCLIEngine passed --tools followed by a separate empty-string token; verified live that this makes the real claude CLI's (2.1.202) variadic tools-list argv parser keep consuming the next token when nothing with a leading '-' follows -- it swallowed the positional prompt itself whenever request.system was unset (my-reporter's summarize path happened to always set system, masking this). Fixed to a single joined '--tools=' token, matching the CLI's own documented empty-string semantics. Existing tests all mocked the Runner boundary and never caught it; strengthened the no-system-prompt test to assert the prompt stays last

Engine model knob: build_engine factory stays a per-tool snippet, NOT a core export, to keep the public API contracts-only/dependency-free; documented cheapest-capable-first in ARCHITECTURE.md (issue #12)

my-changelogger has shipped, so its docs/tools design plan goes historical per the docs/tools/<name>.md convention: added the one-line banner pointing at my-changelogger's own README/CLAUDE.md, matching my-reporter.md's frozen doc exactly. No content otherwise changed

Drafted docs/tools/my-projector.md (fleet GitHub Project board/tracking-issue sync) and docs/tools/my-planner.md (long-horizon multi-item roadmap), both proposed by the user. Resolved the authority boundary against MyOrchestrator up front: MyOrchestrator stays the sole picker of the next action; MyPlanner only feeds it a longer-horizon priority signal; MyProjector makes no priority judgments at all. A fourth 'when to build tool X' agent was considered and rejected as redundant with MyOrchestrator's existing readiness-check + ranking.
